channel.title: Namespace-Rich Blog
channel.description: A feed using Dublin Core and content:encoded

item.title: Understanding Namespaces in RSS
item.dc:creator: Dr. Eleanor Vance
item.dc:subject: Web Standards
item.description: A short summary of the article.
item.content:encoded:
Namespaces in RSS

RSS 2.0 supports extension namespaces for richer metadata. The most common are Dublin Core (dc:) for authorship and dates, and content:encoded for full article HTML.

This lets publishers include both a plain-text summary in <description> and a full HTML body in <content:encoded>.

item.title: Dublin Core Metadata Explained
item.dc:creator: Prof. James Moriarty
item.description: Dublin Core provides a standardized vocabulary for metadata.
item.content:encoded: The Dublin Core Metadata Element Set is a vocabulary of fifteen properties for describing resources. In RSS feeds, the most commonly used elements are dc:creator, dc:date, and dc:subject.
